key_judgments.judgment: Middle East energy shock embeds persistent inflation upside across US and EU bond markets
Global central banks converge on hold amid uncertainty reinforcing higher-for-longer rates
China manufacturing rebound broad-based but cost pressures signal uneven recovery

weekly_brief_draft:
Global macro stress regime shifts to preliminary Amber, driven by inflation and financial stability pressures from Middle East energy shock. US 5-year breakeven inflation hit highest since February 2025, up 26bps since conflict onset, signaling embedded upside risks that pressure Fed easing odds. Futures price 60 percent chance of no rate changes through 2026, with FOMC split 12-19 expecting cuts versus seven holding firm. ECB mirrored caution, holding rates and citing conflict-driven inflation upside via energy alongside growth downside. Brent crude at $112 per barrel and US 10-year yields at 4.43 percent underscore financial market repricing, with European yields at decade highs despite growth headwinds.

Growth signals mixed: US ISM Manufacturing PMI expanded to 52.7 on new orders, consumer confidence ticked to 91.8, but noted tariff costs; China PMIs rebounded broadly to 50.4 official and 50.8 private, hinting stabilization though input costs rise. EU lags with Spain PMI contracting to 48.7 and UK revised to 51.0 amid output decline. Trade domain stable absent new tariffs, though consumer views flag cost impacts. Currency, sovereign debt show no acute stress.

Central bank postures neutral across Fed, ECB, with PBOC unremarked. Divergence potential rises as energy shock tests policy cohesion; LatAm banks turn cautious. No tariff escalations this week.

Tail risks center on sustained oil above $110 triggering prolonged inflation, delaying cuts, with medium probability over 90 days impacting inflation and financial domains. Bond volatility from bank splits looms lower probability near-term. Key judgment: energy shock persists as structural inflation driver, assessed deteriorating trajectory. China recovery firmer but margin risks emerge. Overall, geopolitical overlay dominates, elevating Amber vigilance without Red triggers across domains.

cross_monitor_candidates.signal: Middle East conflict drives energy shock and global inflation repricing
Oil surge and AI power demand reinforce energy security themes
